faults._note: ADDED — missing scenario. Vehicle dashboard shows 'gun connected' but Spin App shows 0A current during active charging session. Source: EV-AC_LED_Troubleshooting_Matrix.xlsx, State 8 (Charging) resolution.
customerSteps: Confirm the vehicle dashboard shows 'gun connected' or 'charging'.
Open the Spin App and check the current reading while the session is active.
For MG vehicles: ensure the car is locked — MG requires the vehicle to be locked for charging to proceed.
If dashboard shows 'gun connected' and App shows 0A, this is a relay output fault — raise ticket for Power Card replacement.
nocSteps: Check voltage at the output terminal after removing the gun.
Attempt to develop output voltage via command.
If output voltage does not develop to match input voltage → replace Power Card (Littlefuse Relay).
For Panasonic Relay: do NOT replace Power Card — escalate directly to R&D team.
faults.ticketTrigger: App shows 0A during active charging session

faults._note: ADDED — missing scenario. EVSE Suspended state caused by rated current dropping below minimum threshold (6A). Source: EV-AC_LED_Troubleshooting_Matrix.xlsx, State 9 (EVSE Suspended) engineer resolution.
customerSteps: Check the Spin App — if the rated current setting shows less than 6A, the charger will not charge.
Ask the Primary Owner to open the app and verify the rated current is set between 6A and 32A.
Restart the charger after adjusting the rated current. The charger reboots and applies the new value within ~60 seconds.
Reconnect the gun after restart and retry.
nocSteps: Verify current rated-current setting on live params — must be ≥6A.
If rated current is correct, try reinserting or replacing the ribbon cable.
Check all electrical parameters for grid fluctuation.
If issue persists → replace Power Card.
faults.ticketTrigger: Rated current ≥6A but EVSE suspended persists

faults._note: ADDED — missing Tata vehicle-specific EV Suspended scenario. Certain Tata charger manufacturing batches require a KEI gun replacement. Source: EV-AC_LED_Troubleshooting_Matrix.xlsx, State 7 (EV Suspended) resolution.
faults.led: green solid on Tata/Compact (EV suspended state)
customerSteps: Ask the customer for their charger's MFG (manufacturing) month code shown on the label.
If the MFG code is D925 or DO25 — this batch requires a KEI gun replacement. Raise a ticket immediately.
If the vehicle is a Tata Curve: reattach the gun 2–3 times firmly, ensuring it is properly seated. If issue persists, raise a ticket for gun replacement.
For all other vehicles with Tata/Compact charger: check for heat marks or burn signs at the gun terminal and input terminal (photo welcome).
Try restarting the system twice, at least 30 minutes apart. Replug the gun at each restart.
If unresolved after two restarts → raise ticket for engineer visit.
nocSteps: Confirm charger MFG batch code (D925 / DO25 = KEI gun replacement required).
Check CP PWM signal and vehicle authorisation flow.
If vehicle-side issue suspected → advise customer to visit Tata vehicle dealership.
faults.ticketTrigger: MFG code D925/DO25 confirmed, or restarts fail to resolve
